findingDetails.title: classifySaveErrorCode over-broad substring routing for path-safety errors (E089)
findingDetails.evidence: E089 branch matches lower.includes('access denied')||'path must not contain'||'traversal'. 'access denied' is a generic substring that can appear in OS-level permission/ACL errors that are not validation failures, misclassifying them E089 (validation) and sending callers down the wrong recovery path. DB branch (511-520) is correctly ordered before it, but any message literally containing 'access denied' is captured by the validation branch.
findingDetails.recommendation: Tighten path-safety matches to anchored/contextual tokens (e.g. require a path-safety prefix) or add a permission/EACCES branch before E089 so OS permission errors do not collapse into validation.

findingDetails.title: Two parallel divergent SIGTERM/SIGINT handler stacks — inconsistent process exit code on signal
findingDetails.evidence: shutdown-hooks.ts installs its own process.once SIGTERM/SIGINT -> exit(143|130); context-server installs process.on SIGTERM/SIGINT -> fatalShutdown -> exit(0). Both fire; re-entrancy guards (shuttingDown 1559, running 63) prevent hook double-run but exit code is non-deterministic.
findingDetails.recommendation: Consolidate signal handling to one stack; have fatalShutdown own the exit code (use 143/130 to match convention) or have shutdown-hooks defer to the daemon path.

findingDetails.title: Socket bind path never canonicalized before listen()/chmod — symlink at socket tail is followed, bypassing the lstat unlink-hijack fence (tail-symlink TOCTOU)
findingDetails.evidence: path.resolve(options.socketPath) at :238 does not dereference symlinks; canonicalizePath (:77-99) realpaths only the existing ancestor and re-appends the raw SOCKET_FILE_NAME tail; chmodSync(socketPath,0o600) at :355 follows symlinks; the lstat fence (canUnlinkExistingSocket :163) is only consulted on the EADDRINUSE reclaim branch (:335), not on fresh bind (:308). /tmp + os.tmpdir() are allowed roots (:118-124) and /tmp is world-writable+sticky, so a same-host local attacker can pre-create the symlink tail before the daemon binds.
findingDetails.recommendation: realpath/lstat the full socket path (reject isSymbolicLink) on the fresh-bind path too, not only on reclaim; use open+fchmod no-follow for the 0o600 step; or require a daemon-owned 0700 socket dir (never bare /tmp).
findingDetails.findingClass: defect (toctou / symlink-following on privileged fs op)
findingDetails.scopeProof: socket-server.ts in-range (hardened by 3d1667dd68); line 238 path.resolve no realpath; line 355 chmod follows; lstat only at 163 gated behind EADDRINUSE 335; code-graph fork diff -q BYTE-IDENTICAL.
findingDetails.affectedSurfaceHints: fresh-bind tail canonicalization; chmod no-follow; daemon-owned socket dir contract; both shared + code-graph copies (compounds F-003).

findingDetails.title: validateFolder runs an unbounded DFS readFileSync over the entire .opencode/specs tree on every strict validation — DoS amplification
findingDetails.evidence: collectKnownSessionIds rewinds to the .opencode/specs root (:293-297) and DFS-walks the whole subtree readFileSync-ing every *.md (:302-317) with no node/time/depth cap (only node_modules/.git/scratch pruned at :311); called unconditionally for FRONTMATTER_MEMORY_BLOCK on every validateFolder (:380); validateFolder is also exported programmatically (api/index.ts:78-83), widening the trigger surface beyond the CLI.
findingDetails.recommendation: cap nodes/files/wall-clock, short-circuit once all parent_session_ids resolve, or scope the walk to the nearest packet ancestor instead of the whole specs root.
findingDetails.scopeProof: orchestrator.ts in-range; walk loop 300-319 has no count/time/depth guard; collectKnownSessionIds called on every validateFolder via line 380; programmatic export at api/index.ts:78-83.
findingDetails.affectedSurfaceHints: walk bounding; short-circuit on lineage resolution; scope to packet ancestor; ties to the api/index.ts programmatic export trigger surface.

findingDetails.title: canonicalizePath swallows realpath failures and falls back to non-canonical resolved path — fail-open weakens isWithinAllowedSocketRoot containment
findingDetails.fileLine: shared/ipc/socket-server.ts:94-98 (consumed by resolveIpcSocketPath :134-139); byte-identical code-graph fork
findingDetails.evidence: canonicalizePath wraps realpathSync.native in a bare try/catch that returns the literal path.resolve output on any non-ENOENT failure (:94-98); isWithinAllowedSocketRoot (:126-128) is the sole containment gate and prefix-compares a possibly-non-canonical candidate against canonicalized roots, so an EACCES/EIO realpath failure on a symlinked ancestor can let a candidate pass (or wrongly fail) the boundary.
findingDetails.recommendation: fail closed (reject bind) on realpath failure rather than returning the non-canonical path; distinguish ENOENT (already handled by the existence loop) from EACCES/other.
findingDetails.scopeProof: in-range; bare catch returns resolved at 96-98; isWithinAllowedSocketRoot is the only gate (126-128) consuming it at 134-135; code-graph fork byte-identical.
findingDetails.affectedSurfaceHints: fail-closed canonicalization; ENOENT vs EACCES handling; same root weakness as F-A5-01 (incomplete canonicalization).

findingDetails.title: Shutdown durability cluster compounds under concurrent-session SIGTERM: exit-stack race (F-A4-02) can bypass the ordered drain, leaving the unfenced ingest worker (F-A4-01) with a dirty WAL + socket on the reclaim branch (F-A4-03 inter F-A5-01)
findingDetails.evidence: context-server.ts:1681-1692 fatalShutdown->exit0 AND shutdown-hooks.ts:129-148 own process.once->exitProcess 143/130 both fire on SIGTERM; runShutdownHooks is step 9 of fatalShutdown (1607-1609), the standalone stack does NOT run the fileWatcher-first/closeDb-after drain; 5000ms Promise.race force-kill (1624-1628); job-queue.ts:746-752 fire-and-forget drainQueue no shuttingDown guard (F-A4-01); socket-server.ts:357-388 non-re-entrant leak (F-A4-03); fence only on EADDRINUSE reclaim (335, F-A5-01 coupling); concurrent-session premise = Known Context
findingDetails.recommendation: Collapse to ONE SIGTERM/SIGINT handler stack (let fatalShutdown own it; shutdown-hooks registers a hook not its own process.once) — keystone fix that retroactively bounds F-A4-01; add ingest worker to cleanup drain before closeDb; dispose prior socket server on re-init
findingDetails.scopeProof: all cited lines in-range A4/A5 targets (iters 5,6); concurrent-session premise documented Known Context; synthesizes F-A4-01+F-A4-02+F-A4-03+F-A5-01
affectedSurfaceHints: collapse to one SIGTERM/SIGINT handler stack (F-A4-02 keystone)
dispose prior socket server on re-init (F-A4-03)
findingDetails.claim: On a concurrent-session SIGTERM, the two divergent signal-handler stacks race to exit; if the bare shutdown-hooks stack wins it skips fatalShutdown's ordered drain, so the unfenced ingest worker (F-A4-01) can leave a dirty WAL with no closeDb checkpoint while the non-re-entrant socket leak (F-A4-03) forces the next respawn onto the EADDRINUSE reclaim branch — worse than any single A4 finding.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Confirmed shutdown-hooks' own handler does NOT run the ordered drain (calls handleShutdownSignal->exitProcess directly); re-entrancy guards (shuttingDown 1559, running 63) prevent double-RUN of hooks but do not arbitrate which stack reaches process.exit first; crash-recovery bounds impact to recoverable (caps at P1).
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Ingest queue usually idle at shutdown and fatalShutdown almost always wins the race, so the compound rarely materializes. Partially accepted (P1 recoverable, not P0) — but concurrent-session context makes a mid-job SIGTERM reachable and the exit-stack race is genuinely nondeterministic.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Drop toward P2 if a single-stack fix lands, or if the ingest worker is provably stopped before closeDb, or if jobs are contractually idle at shutdown.
findingDetails.skepticPass: Skeptic: 'just 3 findings re-stated?' Referee: NO — the NEW fact is the exit-stack RACE (F-A4-02) can BYPASS the drain F-A4-01's recoverability assumed; emergent at cross-cutting level. P1 ceiling held, reported as compound not re-count.
